content.full_text:
FPPC
Jun 27 @ 8:9 PM 90
June 22, 1990

George F. Galvan
14825 El Camino Real
Atascadero, CA 93422

Fair Political Practices
Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Dear Sirs:

Enclosed are copies of a Republican Voters Guide that was mailed to every Republican voter in San Luis Obispo County, and depending upon your address varied with the names of Republican Central Committee candidates. This voters guide Mailed by the Young Republicans of San Luis Obispo and Robert Tapella, who is employed by Congressman William Thomas, on June 20, told me that he did author the guide. Appearing on the guide is Tapella's home telephone number on the mailer. Tapella works out of Congressman Thomas's Pismo Beach office.

Enclosed is another mailer entitled Non-Partisan Voting Guide that was also received by Republican voters in this County. One of the candidates listed on both mailers told me that Tapella approached him by offering to have his name placed on the Non-Partisan Voting Guide, but he told Tapella that he did not have the money to pay for that service and was told by Tapella that the Young Republicans would pay for placing his name on the mailer.

I have several questions concerning both mailers. Are mailers that look like official ballots permissable to be sent out to voters? Also, are Federal employees allowed to engage in raising money and engaging in this type of political activity? If these activities are not illegal they should be and if they are I feel that Tapella should be held accountable in some way. Any information you can provide me concerning this type of activity will be appreciated.

Sincerely,

George F. Galvan

NONPARTISAN BALLOT
COUNTY OF SAN LUIS OBISPO
TUESDAY, JUNE 5, 1990

108 PASSENGER RAIL AND CLEAN AIR BOND ACT OF 1990.
We support Governor Deukemjian in his innovative and
comprehensive transportation plan.

YES
NO +

109 GOVERNOR'S REVIEW OF LEGISLATION. Will result in
better government. We join Governor Deukemjian and State
Senator Ken Maddy in urging a YES vote.

YES
NO +

110 PROPERTY TAX EXEMPTION FOR SEVERELY DISABL-
ED PERSONS. Will provide much needed tax relief for our severely
disabled neighbors.

YES
NO +

111 THE TRAFFIC CONGESTION RELIEF AND SPENDING
LIMITATION ACT OF 1990. Will enact a statewide traffic relief pro-
gram that is innovative and comprehensive. We join Governor
Deukemjian in recommending a YES vote.

YES
NO +

114 MURDER OF A PEACE OFFICER. Will update and clarify
provisions regarding the murder of peace officers.

YES
NO +

115 CRIMINAL LAW. Will remedy gross inequities in the law
and bring more violent criminals to justice. We join U.S. Senator
Pete Wilson in recommending a YES vote.

YES
NO +

117 WILDLIFE PROTECTION. We join the State Board of
Forestry and the State Fish and Game Commission in opposing this
proposition.

YES
NO +

119 REAPPORTIONMENT
BY COMMISSION. Will provide a
fundamental change in the
redistricting process and WILL
STOP GERRYMANDERING. We
join President Reagan in urging
a YES vote.

YES
NO +

120 NEW PRISON CONSTRUCTION. Will provide funds needed
to build new prisons to help us rid our streets of dangerous criminals
from the streets. We join Governor Deukemjian in recommending
a YES vote.

YES
NO +

121 HIGHER EDUCATION FACILITIES BOND ACT OF JUNE
1990. Will provide funding to maintain and improve our public
colleges and universities. We join Governor Deukemjian in recom-
mending a YES vote.

YES
NO +

122 EARTHQUAKE SAFETY AND PUBLIC BUILDINGS
REHABILITATION BOND ACT OF 1990. Will help strengthen vital
community buildings that provide fire and police protection and
disaster response during emergencies.

YES
NO +

123 SCHOOL FACILITIES BOND ACT. We join Governor
Deukemjian because our children deserve an adequate
learning environment.

YES
NO +

---

Republican Pocket Voters Guide-KEEPER
Take me with you Tuesday-June 5th

San Luis Obispo County Young Republicans
Political Action Committee
Post Office Box 937, San Luis Obispo, California 93406

For information concerning your polling place
or ride to the polls, please call: 549-5223
Insurance in voting or ride to polls open at 8:00 p.m.
Polls open at 7:00 a.m.

U.S. Senator Pete Wilson

[PHOTOGRAPH: Black and white headshot of Pete Wilson]

Young
Guide to     Candidates

GOVERNOR

PETE WILSON ➜ ⊙

LIEUTENANT GOVERNOR

MARIAN BERGESON ➜ ⊙
A conservative leader of principle, Marian Bergeson has been rated by her peers as the Republican State Senator with the highest integrity. She supports the death penalty and is the only candidate for Lieutenant Governor who backed Proposition 13 from the very start. She is a recognized leader for quality education and better schools. Truly the conservative's choice.*

SECRETARY OF STATE

JOAN MILKE FLORES ➜ ⊙
A Republican leader on the Los Angeles City Council. A State delegate for Pete Wilson for Governor. Appointed as a Delegate to the Republican National Convention by President George Bush. Her opponent in the Republican primary is a former Democrat, and was a losing Democrat candidate for Senate and the Los Angeles City Council. We join with the California Young Republicans and the California Congress of Republicans in urging your vote for Joan Mike Flores, a life-long Republican.*

TREASURER

ANGELA "BAY" BUCHANAUR ➜ ⊙
Appointed by President Ronald Reagan as our youngest United States Treasurer. Reagan/Bush chief financial officer. A woman of integrity, she has refused to accept campaign contributions from any special interests that do business with the State Treasurer's office. We join with the California Republican Assembly and the California Young Republicans in urging you to vote for Bay Buchanan . . . a genuine conservative leader.*

INSURANCE COMMISSIONER

JOHN S. PARISE ➜ ⊙
He will be the Insurance Commissioner of the people . . . not the insurance companies. A WW II Purple Heart Marine who was at Iwo Jima aboard the USS Saratoga. Degree in economics from USC. With 40 years' insurance experience, 20 years' legal experience, and as a businessman, he knows how to negotiate the best deal for the consumer. Has the training and experience to untangle the insurance mess. The Republicans' choice.*

UNITED STATES REPRESENTATIVE

JERRY M. REISS*
16th District
Member of Congress

WILLIAM M. THOMAS*
20th District
Member of Congress

➜ ⊙

➜ ⊙

MEMBER STATE SENATE

KENNETH L. MADDY ➜ ⊙
14th District
State Senator, 14th District

MEMBER STATE ASSEMBLY

ERIC SEASTRAND ➜ ⊙
29th District
Member of the California State Assembly, 29th District

SAN LUIS OBISPO COUNTY
REPUBLICAN CENTRAL COMMITTEE

Supervisorial District 1
TOM MARTIN* Winery Owner
HARRY L. OVITT* First District Supervisor
JOHN W. LATHROPE JR.* Certified Public Accountant
Supervisorial District 3
DENNIS V. BROWN* Administrator
ROGER FREBERG* Incumbent
GRACE DILLON* Retired
BRYAN P. DUVALL* Investment Banker

Supervisorial District 4
RUTH E. BRICKETTI* Incumbent
SUE MASON* Businesswoman
JOHN M. TAYLOR* Farmer
LIVIA P. SEIMI* Incumbent

Supervisorial District 5
MICHAEL C. WHITEFORD* Businessman
JERRY M. REISS* Businessman/City Councilmember
MARGARITE GOWGANI* Incumbent
GEORGE GOWGANI* Incumbent

YES ON
PROPOSITION 119

Proposition 119 is the initiative that the Democrats fear the most.
119 will prevent them from carving up our state through legislative districts were their own private kingdoms.

Join with President Reagan and other Republican leaders and vote YES on 119.

BOARD OF EQUALIZATION

CLAUDE W. PARRISH* ➜ ⊙
2nd District

COUNTY SUPERVISOR

BILL COY* ➜ ⊙
2nd District
Incumbent

SLO-A29

Summary of
Propositions                                    Endorsements

_______________________________________________

Traffic Congestion Relief Initiative

111-YES Governor Deukmejian, the Republican Party,
California Taxpayers Association, California
108-YES Association of Highway Patrolmen, Califor-
nia Seniors Coalition and more than 100
statewide organizations URGE A YES VOTE to reduce traffic,
provide earthquake-safe highways, fix potholes and expand rail
transit. Requires annual state audit to ensure funds are spent
only on transportation improvements.*

Senator Wilson's Crime Initiative

115-YES Toughens death penalty law by overturning
decisions by the Rose bird Court; decreases
the length and cost of criminal trials; and closes a legal loop-
hole that allowed a convicted rapist, who brutally tortured his
victim, to be set free after serving only 7½ years. We join
U.S. Senator Pete Wilson and the California District Attorney's
Association in urging a YES vote.

Land Acquisition Initiative

117-NO Diverts badly needed tax dollars away from
critical existing programs like health care
and endangered species protection, to purchase land. We join
with the California Wildlife Federation, California Optometric
of Local Health Officials, California Taxpayers Association and
Republican Senator Robert Beverly in urging a NO vote.*

Reapportionment Initiative

119-YES Sponsored by San Mateo County Supervisor
Tom Huening, this is the initiative the politi-
cians most want to defeat. Takes the drawing of legislative
district lines out of the hands of politicians. Creates a citizens'
panel to select a fair redistricting plan for California. The only
reapportionment initiative that has been endorsed by President
Ronald Reagan and the League of Women Voters. Vote YES.*

School Facilities Bond Act

123-YES Will provide the funding local school dis-
tricts need to build classrooms for our
rapidly growing, school-age population. We join with Governor

107   NO          113   YES        119  *YES
108  *YES        114   YES         120   YES
109   YES        115   YES         121   YES
110   YES        116  *YES        122   NO
111  *YES        117  *NO         123  *YES
112   YES        118   YES

Governor.................................................PETE WILSON
Lieutenant Governor.............................*MARIAN BERGESON
Secretary of State...............................*JOAN MILKE FLORES
Controller..........................................MATT FONG
Treasurer.........................................*ANGELA "BAY" BUCHANAN
..........................................................DANIEL E. LUNGREN
Insurance Commissioner...........................*JOHN S. PARISE
Superintendent of Public Instruction........SAMUEL RODRIGUEZ


The Non-Partisan Candidate
Evaluation Council

We are a statewide non-profit volunteer or-
ganization comprised of active and responsible
conservative Republicans who believe in self-
reliance and less government intervention.

The purpose of the VOTING GUIDE is to in-
form voters which candidates for public office
most closely reflect our conservative principles.

The Council also takes positions on ballot
measures, and our reasons for support or oppo-
sition are clearly stated in the VOTING GUIDE.

The VOTING guide has been published at
election time for over 26 years.

Vote June 5 and take your VOTING GUIDE to
the polls with you.


NOTICE TO VOTERS

THIS DOCUMENT WAS PREPARED BY THE NON-PARTISAN CANDI-
DATE EVALUATION COUNCIL, INC., NOT AN OFFICIAL POLITICAL
PARTY ORGANIZATION. Appearance on this mailer does not neces-
sarily imply endorsement of others in this mailer. Appearance is
paid for and authorized by each candidate and ballot measure
which is designated by an *.

Dear Republican Friends — We have studied carefully the candidates for Republican office and the ballot
measures and recommend the following. Please support these excellent Republican candidates. Mark your
ballot carefully to avoid error.

—YOU MAY TAKE THIS CARD TO THE POLLS WITH YOU—
San Luis Obispo County Young Republicans

                                                    A MEASURE A is all concerned and unair. It ties County growth
                                                      to a State [illegible] San Jose and Los Angeles. It was written in secret
                                                      by a special interest group and does not allow for public input. We
                                                      support local control of growth and urge a NO vote on Measure A.

                                                    YES

Box 807 Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 Dear Sirs: Enclosed are copies of a Republican Voters Guide that was mailed to every Republican voter in San Luis Obispo County, and depending upon your address varied with the names of Republican Central Committee candidates. This voters guide Mailed by the Young Republicans of San Luis Obispo and Robert Tapella, who is employed by Congressman William Thomas, on June 20, told me that he did author the guide. Appearing on the guide is Tapella's home telephone number on the mailer. Tapella works out of Congressman Thomas's Pismo Beach office. Enclosed is another mailer entitled Non-Partisan Voting Guide that was also received by Republican voters of this County. One of the candidates listed on both mailers told me that Tapella approached him by offering to have his name placed on the Non-Partisan Voting Guide, but he told Tapella that he did not have the money to pay for that service and was told by Tapella that the Young Republicans would pay for placing his name on the mailer. I have several questions concerning both mailers. Are mailers that look like official ballots permissable to be sent out to voters? Also, are Federal employees allowed to engage in raising money and engaging in this type of political activity? If these activities are not illegal the should be and if they are I feel that Tapella should be held accountable in some way. Any information you can provide me concerning this type of activity will be appreciated. Sincerely, [signature] George E. Galvan The image is a nonpartisan ballot for the County of San Luis Obispo, dated Tuesday, June 5, 1990. The ballot lists several propositions with "YES" and "NO" checkboxes for each. Here are the propositions and their summaries: 108. PASSENGER RAIL AND CLEAN AIR BOND ACT OF 1990. We support Governor Deukmejian in his innovative and comprehensive transportation plan. 109. GOVERNOR'S REVIEW OF LEGISLATION. Will result in better government. We join Governor Deukmejian and State Senator Ken Maddy in urging a YES vote. 110. PROPERTY TAX EXEMPTION FOR SEVERELY DISABLED PERSONS. Will provide much needed tax relief for our severely disabled neighbors. 111. THE TRAFFIC CONGESTION RELIEF AND SPENDING LIMITATION ACT OF 1990. Will enact a statewide traffic relief program that is innovative and comprehensive. We join Governor Deukmejian in recommending a YES vote. 114. MURDER OF A PEACE OFFICER. Will update and clarify provisions regarding the murder of peace officers. 115. CRIMINAL LAW. Will remedy gross inequities in the law and bring more violent criminals to justice. We join U.S. Senator Pete Wilson in recommending a YES vote. 117. WILDLIFE PROTECTION. We join the State Board of Forestry and the State Fish and Game Commission in opposing this proposition. 119. REAPPORTIONMENT BY COMMISSION. Will provide a fundamental change in the redistricting process and WILL STOP GERRYMANDERING. We join President Reagan in urging a YES vote. 120. NEW
